summary: Descriptive overview of marriageformation in substantive law: license requirements, solemnization, common law marriage.
key_points: license requirements is a fundamental concept in family law.
Legal standards for marriageformation are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine marriageformation in litigation.
The doctrine of marriageformation has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding marriageformation requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
